title: Intimate Partner Violence
description: Percentage of women aged 15 to 49 years who have ever had a husband or intimate partner who have ever experienced emotional, physical, or sexual violence committed by their current or most recent husband/intimate partner. Source: Philippine Statistics Authority, 2025 National Demographic and Health Survey.
subtitle: Percentage of women aged 15 to 49 years who have ever had a husband or intimate partner who have ever experienced emotional, physical, or sexual violence committed by their current or most recent husband/intimate partner, according to background characteristics: Philippines, 2022 and 2025

footnotes: ² Region IX includes the province of Sulu in 2025.
“NA” denotes that an estimate is not reported.
"0.0" indicates that an estimate is less than 0.05 percent.

The term husband includes a partner with whom a woman is living as if married. Husband/intimate partner refers to the current husband for currently married women, the most recent husband for divorced, separated or widowed women, the current intimate partner for never married women who currently have an intimate partner, and the most recent intimate partner for never married women who do not currently have an intimate partner but had one in the past.
Details may not add up to total due to rounding.
dataTables.source: Philippine Statistics Authority, 2022 and2025 National Demographic and Health Survey

sections.heading: Reported IPV indicators declined from 2022
sections.body: Among women aged 15 to 49 who ever had a husband or intimate partner, physical or sexual or emotional violence was 15.4 percent in 2025, down from 17.5 percent in 2022. Physical or sexual violence fell from 7.3 to 5.8 percent. Emotional violence remains the largest component at 14.0 percent, compared with 5.3 percent for physical violence and 1.8 percent for sexual violence.

sections.heading: Emotional violence drives the summary measure
sections.body: The summary indicator for physical or sexual or emotional violence is 15.4 percent, while emotional violence alone is 14.0 percent. Physical violence is 5.3 percent and sexual violence is 1.8 percent. This means the broad IPV measure is primarily shaped by emotional violence, though physical and sexual violence remain important components.

sections.heading: Caraga and Bicol are the highest regional points
sections.body: Caraga had the highest physical or sexual or emotional violence at 24.0 percent, closely followed by Bicol Region at 23.9 percent. Zamboanga Peninsula was also high at 21.1 percent. For physical or sexual violence, Bicol Region led at 10.2 percent, followed by Davao Region at 9.3 percent and MIMAROPA at 9.0 percent.

sections.heading: BARMM is lowest on the reported IPV measures
sections.body: BARMM had the lowest physical or sexual or emotional violence at 3.2 percent and the lowest physical or sexual violence at 0.9 percent. Central Visayas and Negros Island Region were also low on the summary measure, at 9.2 and 11.7 percent. These regional differences are much wider than the national change from 2022 to 2025.

sections.heading: Marital status shows the sharpest non-regional contrast
sections.body: Women who were divorced, separated, or widowed had the highest physical or sexual or emotional violence at 41.4 percent. Women who had an intimate partner but did not currently have one were also high at 33.4 percent, while women who currently had an intimate partner were at 7.4 percent. This is the strongest non-regional difference in the page.

sections.heading: Wealth differences are clearer than age differences
sections.body: Physical or sexual or emotional violence was 19.1 percent in the lowest wealth quintile and 10.6 percent in the highest. Age differences were much smaller, ranging from 14.3 percent among women aged 20-24 to 16.1 percent among women aged 40-49. Wealth and marital status therefore separate the data more strongly than age.

takeaways: The broad IPV measure declined from 17.5 percent in 2022 to 15.4 percent in 2025.
Emotional violence is the largest component of the IPV summary measure.
Caraga and Bicol Region have the highest reported levels on the broad IPV measure, while BARMM is lowest.
Marital status is the sharpest non-regional contrast, with divorced, separated, or widowed women reporting much higher IPV.
